---
title: "Scaling and Panning"
format: 
  closeread-html:
    theme: cosmo
    css: ../../styles.css
    cr-style:
      narrative-background-color-sidebar: "#fcf7f0"
      section-background-color: white
      narrative-text-color-sidebar: black
      narrative-font-size: 2em
---

::::{.cr-section}

## Scaling

A graph without any visual effects at a normal scale. @cr-plot

:::{#cr-plot}
```{r barchart8, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)

# number of storms per year (1980-2022)
storms_per_year = storms |>
  filter(year >= 1980) |> 
  mutate(id = paste0(name, "-", year)) |> 
  count(year, id, name = "count") |> 
  count(year, name = "count")

# color palette
lowcolor = "#dccfb1" # low-light color
medcolor = "#BD835D" # highlight color per decade
maxcolor = "#ff9933" # color of maximum

# all storms with fitted line
storms_per_year |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = count)) +
  geom_col(fill = lowcolor) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, linewidth = 1.4, color = medcolor) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1980, 2020, by = 5)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Number of Storms per Year in the North Atlantic",
       x = "")
```
:::

A graph with a "shrinking" effect. [@cr-plot]{scale-by="0.5"}

A graph with an "enlarging" effect. [@cr-plot]{scale-by="2"}

Now the graph is normal size again. @cr-plot

## Panning

| The panning attribute takes a pair of x and y values that control the horizontal and vertical panning, respectively.
| Normal position 
@cr-plot

Panning to the left [@cr-plot]{pan-to="-100px,0px"}

Panning to the right [@cr-plot]{pan-to="0px,100px"}

Normal position again @cr-plot

::::
